label: Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED REAR with mid-band HALL, LD style — side-split: bedrooms in left column organised as two parallel front-to-rear sub-columns, LDK in right column (living front, dining mid, kitchen rear — stacked, not a combined great room). Left sub-column: [master, ensuite]. Mid sub-column: [standard, hall, common T&B]. Both baths cluster at the rear sharing a wet-stack plumbing wall with the kitchen.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — door on master's rear (north) wall opens north into ensuite. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
master's east wall has a door opening east into the hall — master's only public access. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
standard's north wall has a door opening north into the hall — standard's only public access. Unchanged from the gr sibling.

common bath's south wall has a door opening south into the hall — the bath's only door. Both bedrooms reach the common bath via the hall. DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' door-host group. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
common bath shares plumbing wall with kitchen. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
common bath and ensuite share a plumbing wall — completing the 3-room wet-stack cluster at the rear. Unchanged from the gr sibling.

hall's east wall is an open mouth into dining, not living — the hall sits in the MIDDLE sub-row of its own column (standard front / hall mid / common rear), so its wall has to align with whichever public room occupies that same middle row in the living/dining/kitchen stack. Takes over the role hall<->great played in the gr sibling. Verified empirically: this is a genuinely narrow-band conversion (see notes) unlike the squarish cl_hall_gr->cl_hall_ld sibling, which worked at every size tested.
living and dining stack front-to-rear -- open mouth, no wall (ld style).
dining and kitchen stack front-to-rear -- open pass-through, matches the solver's default kitchen+dining-must-stack rule directly.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_side_split_rear_baths_ld
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Unchanged from the gr sibling. Tried disabling this profile entirely (and forcing it to always apply) while diagnosing the topology's upper feasibility bound -- neither changed the outcome, confirming the profile isn't the binding constraint at the high end (see notes).

notes: Created 2026-07-20 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked
front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of
1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr -- gr sibling deleted the same day,
this topology replaces it. Private column + hall structure entirely
unchanged: two parallel front-to-rear sub-columns (master+ensuite on
the left, standard+hall+common on the mid), bedroom and bath doors all
open onto the hall only, never directly onto the public side.
Layout intent (CANONICAL form -- kitchen on RIGHT, bedrooms on LEFT,
brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'):
Rear band  (1.5 m deep):    [ENSUITE | COMMON T&B | KITCHEN     ]
Middle band (1.5 m deep):   [(master | HALL        | DINING     ]
Front band (3-4 m deep):    [ MASTER | STANDARD    | LIVING     ]
Only ONE adjacency changed role: hall's open-mouth connection moves
from great_room to dining (not living) -- the hall sits in the MIDDLE
sub-row of its own column, so its wall has to align with whichever
public room occupies that same middle row in the living/dining/kitchen
stack. Verified this empirically the same way the squarish
cl_hall_gr->cl_hall_ld conversion was: hall->dining is correct.
living gets hosts_entry instead of great (front-most public room,
same role).
FEASIBILITY -- this conversion is genuinely narrow-band, unlike the
squarish cl_hall_gr->cl_hall_ld sibling (which worked at every size
tested). Diagnostic method: isolated the cause the same way as the
1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_ds_gr LDK investigation (stripped rooms/
flags one at a time against an oversized 25x18 m envelope). Removing
the hall<->dining adjacency alone makes the topology solve at ANY
size; keeping it, the topology solves cleanly (0 warn) from ~11.9x10
(true floor, confirmed by bisection: 11.8x10 and 12x9.7 both
infeasible) through roughly 15x11.5, then goes infeasible for good at
every larger size tested up to 25x18 -- including sizes well past
this topology's own 60-80 sqm floor-area band. Most likely cause:
hall's own width is effectively locked to the middle sub-column's
width (tied to standard's bedroom width via match_bedroom_widths),
which doesn't scale the way it would need to as the envelope grows
for hall to stay aligned with dining's row. Confirmed the compact-
shell profile isn't the cause (tested with it disabled entirely and
forced to always apply -- neither changed the upper bound).
Published minimum: 12x10 (round number just above the true ~11.9x10
floor). 0-warning solve.
gr sibling restored 2026-07-20 (same day as this topology's deletion
and re-creation) once shallow-depth testing (11x9, buildable 7x5)
proved this ld topology genuinely can't reach lots below ~5.5 m
buildable depth -- isolated to the living/dining/kitchen 3-room stack
specifically (confirmed by testing that column alone: needs >=5.5 m
buildable depth even with unlimited width to spread into, while the
bedroom-wing mid sub-column -- standard/hall/common, also 3 rooms --
fits fine at 5 m alone). great_room's 2-deep stack (great+kitchen)
doesn't have this problem, so it's the one to use for shallow lots.
fallback_topology now points to 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr.json
(the restored sibling, same room program) -- deliberately relying on
the general infeasibility-triggered fallback rather than a
fallback_below_buildable_sqm area gate, because the actual binding
constraint is DEPTH specifically, not buildable AREA: widening a
shallow lot does NOT unlock this topology (12x9, 13x9, 15x9 all still
infeasible despite far more area than 12x10), so an area-based
skip-ahead gate would misroute a wide-but-shallow lot straight past a
solve attempt that could never have succeeded anyway, and worse, could
incorrectly gate AWAY a lot that's narrow-but-deep-enough to actually
work. The upper feasibility bound (~15x11.5+) is unaffected by this
restoration and remains unverified against the gr sibling -- gr's own
large-lot behavior wasn't tested this session.
Wet-stack cluster, bedroom/bath width matching, and carport handling
all unchanged from the gr sibling.
claim_dead_strips: true (added 2026-07-20). At tight lot sizes the three
mid-column private rooms (standard 2.2 / hall 2.2-2.8 / common 2.5) and
the two bath widths don't share an edge, so the solve leaves small
unclaimed interior pockets no edge-snap can reach -- e.g. at the 12x10
min, ~0.86 m2 in two pockets. This flag runs the multi-storey pipeline's
claim_dead_strips post-process on the single-storey realize (off by
default catalog-wide): each rectangular dead pocket becomes an L-alcove
on an adjacent room. At 12x10 living absorbs the 0.78 m2 pocket (L-shaped
living) and hall takes a 0.07 m2 sliver -> 0 dead space, still 0 warnings.
Purely a realized-geometry cleanup -- never touches solver feasibility, so
the feasible band and minimums above are unaffected. At generous widths
(14x10+) the private rooms tile evenly on their own and the flag is inert.
The gr sibling (1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr) does NOT set this flag
yet -- it has its own, larger dead space (~2.4 m2 at 12x10) but was left
as-is since only this topology's output was flagged for cleanup.
